articles.title: People Pretending to Be Gay for Asylum
articles.summary: The BBC found that some law firms and advisers are helping migrants pretend to be gay to stay in the UK. Migrants pay a lot of money for fake stories and evidence, like letters and photos, to claim asylum. They say they are gay and afraid to go back to countries like Pakistan or Bangladesh, where being gay is against the law. The UK offers protection to people in danger, but this system is being misused. The Home Office warns that anyone cheating will face legal trouble, including being sent away. An undercover investigation showed advisers helping students with expired visas make false claims at events like a support group meeting in London.

articles.title: Reporters Pretend to Be Gay for Asylum Help
articles.summary: A BBC investigation found that some immigration advisers are helping migrants cheat the asylum system in the UK. They tell migrants to pretend to be gay to get asylum. A BBC reporter met an adviser named Tanisa Khan, who offered to give fake evidence, like letters and photos, to make it look like the reporter was gay. Migrants then apply for asylum, saying they are scared to go back to countries like Pakistan or Bangladesh. The Home Office says people who cheat will be punished and could be removed from the UK. This story shows how some people try to trick the system to stay in the UK.

articles.title: A Court Decides About Sharing Mining Money
articles.summary: Gina Rinehart is the richest person in Australia, and she owns big mines that dig up iron ore, a metal used to make things like cars and buildings. She got these mines from her dad a long time ago. Some people, including two of her children and the family of her dad's old business partner, said they should get some of the money from the mines. A court in Australia recently decided that Gina must share some of the money, called royalties, with these people. Royalties are payments made when someone uses something you own, like a mine. The court said Gina can keep owning the mines, but she has to give part of the money to the others. This case was about a very big mine called Hope Downs, and it took over 13 years for the court to make this decision. The judge said both sides won and lost some parts of the argument.

articles.title: A Big Budget Plan for the Military
articles.summary: President Trump wants to spend a lot more money on the military in 2027. He asked for $1.5 trillion for defense, which is 44% higher than before. This is the biggest request in many years. At the same time, he plans to spend less on other programs like schools and roads by 10%. A man named Russell Vought, who works on the budget, talked to Congress about this plan. He said the president wants to keep America safe. The budget shows what the president thinks is important, but Congress gets to decide if they will use it. They often change the budget. You can watch the talk live online.

articles.title: People in Tehran Talk About War and Peace
articles.summary: In Tehran, the capital of Iran, people are talking a lot about war and peace. A ceasefire, which is a temporary stop to fighting, is ending soon. This makes many Iranians feel suspenseful because they do not know what will happen next. They wonder if there will be more war or if an agreement can be made. Everyone has different opinions about the conflict, and they discuss it everywhere they go. The city is full of worry and uncertainty as the deadline approaches. This story helps us understand how ordinary people feel during tense times.

articles.title: A Meeting Between Israel and Lebanon
articles.summary: This article talks about a meeting between Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors in Washington, D.C., hosted by U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Barbara Leaf, a former U.S. government official, explains that the meeting is more about showing goodwill than making big changes. She says it is a symbolic start to a process, meaning it is important for building trust but does not solve problems right away. The meeting is fragile, which means it could easily be disrupted. This is part of efforts to address conflicts in the Middle East, where Israel and Lebanon have had tensions. The U.S. is helping by bringing them together to talk, hoping it leads to more discussions in the future.
